programme.title: Anthony Bourdain: No Reservations
programme.desc: Unknown foods of Colombia; seafood in Cartagena; market workers in Cecilia; the island of Tierra Bomba; fried pig intestines and boiled pig ears.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain: No Reservations
programme.desc: Anthony visits the city of Belém in Brazil; he immerses himself in the unexplored gastronomical treasures of the Amazon rainforest; he finds a prehistoric delicacy, the pirarucu.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain: No Reservations
programme.desc: Tony travels to Cajun Country to dine with some locals and eat at a crawfish boil to see what sets the bayou apart from the mainland.

programme.title: Man Fire Food
programme.desc: Leatha's Bar-b-cue in Hattiesburg, Mississippi, for pork and beef ribs; Hometown Bar-B-Que in New York for jerk baby back ribs and Korean ribs.

programme.title: Man Fire Food
programme.desc: The 100 year anniversary of St. Mary Magdalene's Church Picnic with several thousand pounds of meat being cooked.

programme.desc: Cardiologist Dr. Ernst von Schwarz, M.D. shows how to manage your blood pressure naturally and help protect your heart for life with My Best Heart.

programme.title: Pain Relief Revealed

programme.title: 1776 - 2026 250th Anniversary Quarter Celebration Program
programme.desc: 2026 will be one of the most historic years ever in U.S. coinage; join your host Mike Mezack to learn about the incredible U.S. Quarter program and get a chance at an exclusive P&D Roll!

programme.title: Roadtrip With G. Garvin
programme.desc: A visit to 610 Magnolia In Louisville, Ky., for slow-cooked oxtails; Hammerheads eclectic menu; Juanita's Burger Boy Diner, serves classic diner fare; The Rudyard Kipling, known for burgoo, a meat and veggie stew.

programme.title: Roadtrip With G. Garvin
programme.desc: A lesson on how to make goat cheese in Franklin, Tennessee; visiting a distillery in Bardstown, Kentucky, to learn about the bourbon process.

programme.title: Roadtrip With G. Garvin
programme.desc: G. Garvin heads back to his hometown of Atlanta; shrimp and grits; red velvet cake; fried chicken.

programme.title: Roadtrip With G. Garvin
programme.desc: The Original Farmer's Market; The Gumbo Pot; Fritzi Dog; Bennett's Ice Cream; The Serving Spoon.

programme.title: Cheap Eats
programme.desc: Ali Khan discovers a variety of Austin food fare; migas tacos at a popular roadside stop; authentic Texas barbecue; a bayou experience right out of MawMaw's recipe book.

programme.title: Cheap Eats
programme.desc: Ali Khan tries fried chicken, biscuits, eggs and bacon for breakfast; sampling an Italian beef sandwich for lunch; trying a chocolate almond croissant; enjoying gourmet mac and cheese.

programme.desc: Ali Khan discovers more in Albuquerque than just the hype; he samples a vegan waffle, green chile cheeseburgers, a designer donut and carne adovada.

programme.desc: Ali Khan visits Pittsburgh for some cheesy pancakes and takes on a sub that might blow the classic Philly cheesesteak out of the water; Ali discovers the secrets to Pittsburgh's best pretzels and ends the day at one of the city's hottest new spots.

programme.desc: Ali visits Baltimore and comes face-to-face with bacon-infused pancakes and has his first taste of Maryland pit beef; Ali snacks on Basque-style fried cheese balls before he tries some Korean-style street noodles.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew & 'Que

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations
programme.desc: Andrew explores the classic comfort foods and modern recipes of Porto, Portugal, the City of Bridges; he tries dried, salty cod baked into a creamy casserole, white bean and tripe stew, octopus rice, and Port wine.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations
programme.desc: Andrew highlights Tuscan specialties like small-batch extra virgin olive oil, spicy fish stew served over crusty bread, chickpea pancakes and whole fish simmered quickly in spicy tomato sauce.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations
programme.desc: Andrew explores the region of Emilia-Romagna, Italy, discovering ancient recipes and culinary classics including mortadella sausage, parmigiano reggiano and tortellini in broth.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern checks out the inventive and international eats of New Jersey, including all-you-can-eat Brazilian meat, rich seafood stew, potato-loaded hot dogs, and Dominican marinated pork.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations
programme.desc: Andrew explores the iconic dishes of picturesque Nova Scotia, Canada; there, the proximity to the sea, which is never more than 67 kilometers away, influences the local cuisine.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations
programme.desc: Andrew reveals what makes the food scene in Krakow, Poland, delicious; the city's edible symbols take the form of pierogies, hearty stuffed cabbage rolls and whole-roasted pork knuckle.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Unique Sweets
programme.desc: Featuring french pastry shops in Los Angeles; the kouign-amann, a cake with pistachios and raspberries of Breton origin.

programme.title: Unique Sweets
programme.desc: At Dominique Ansel Kitchen, desserts are made to order; at Good Batch, customers go wild for treats like the deconstructed candy bar called the Macaroon Ice Cream Sandwich; Snow Days provides desserts that bring out the kid in everyone.

programme.title: Food Paradise
programme.desc: Diners make mouthwatering memories at monumental sites, from sandwiches stacked with international flavors across the street from the National Zoo in Washington, D.C., to silky, sweet ice cream sundaes near Golden Gate Park in San Francisco.

programme.title: Man's Greatest Food
programme.desc: From the Atlantic seaboard to the Pacific coast, down deep in the bayou and high in the Rockies, Roger travels America to reel in the fifteen very best seafood dishes.

programme.title: Man's Greatest Food
programme.desc: Roger Mooking travels across America in search of the 12 best chicken dishes; from frying and baking to grilling, Roger hangs out with chefs as they explore all the best ways to cook wings, pot pies, pizzas and other savory chicken dishes.

programme.title: Carnival Eats
programme.desc: Ingenious fair vendors use the humble potato to create some amazing and original midway food offerings.

programme.title: Carnival Eats
programme.desc: Bacon and Bourbon Fest in Wellington, Fla.; Stuffed Crust Bacon-Za; Peanut Butter Pork Donuts that hit the sweet and salty spot; ThurtenE Carnival at Washington University in St. Louis, where he gets his fill of delicious school spirit.

programme.title: Carnival Eats
programme.desc: Noah Cappe heads to the Greater Baton Rouge State Fair for a helping of Cajun treats, like Cajun boudin tacos and pecan pie pops; basking in the lights of the Aurora Winter Festival in Toronto.

programme.title: Carnival Eats
programme.desc: Noah Cappe dives into the fairground for some of the yummiest seafood snacks on the midway.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern explores the neighborhoods that make Chicago a culinary treasure trove, from handcrafted burgers in the West Loop to street-style tacos in Wicker Park and barbecue chicken served up in Bronzeville.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations
programme.desc: Andrew dives into the adventurous city of Zagreb in Croatia; the city's cuisine has a meat-focused menu and celebrates iconic dishes.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern uncovers the boardwalk indulgences of the Jersey Shore; from sticky-sweet saltwater taffy to slices of tomato pie and Italian subs, the Jersey Shore's culinary scene is a carnival for the taste buds.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations
programme.desc: Andrew samples traditional dishes in Bangkok including steamed whole bass, massaman curry and more.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern is in El Paso, Texas, to dive into its rich food culture; he tries everything from traditional fajitas and menudo to rolled tacos and entomatadas.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern highlights the local favorites in central New York, finding beef and veal sausages at one of America's oldest hot dog stands, half-moon cookies that predate New York City's iconic black and white cookie and more.

programme.desc: Casey Webb visits Wilmington, N.C., for a barbecue bonanza, a crazy burrito and a big, bad behemoth in the undefeated Pierogi Team Challenge.

programme.desc: Casey Webb's appetizing adventures take him to Hoboken, N.J.; he munches on homemade mozzarella, takes a bite out of a bagel behemoth and comes face-to-face with a slice of pure fire.
